Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(monitor): sesiones de agentes en worktrees ahora visibles en dashboard (#1248)

**Problema**:
- Dashboard y /monitor mostraban 0 agentes activos aunque 5 estuvieran corriendo en worktrees
- Causa: dashboard-server.js leía sesiones del worktree (copia estática), no del repo principal

**Cambios**:
1. dashboard-server.js: resolver REPO_ROOT via `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` (como activity-logger.js)
2. dashboard-server.js: filtro zombie alineado con SKILL.md (30min para no-sprint, nunca para sprint)
3. dashboard-server.js: eliminar filtro stale innecesario en panel EJECUCIÓN
4. SKILL.md: actualizar instrucciones de /monitor para resolver REPO_ROOT antes de leer sesiones

**Resultado**:
✅ Las 5 sesiones de agentes en worktrees ahora son visibles en SESIONES
✅ Panel EJECUCIÓN muestra progreso correcto, stale sessions (15-30min) siguen visibles
✅ Criterios de aceptación: type:parent + status:active + <30min → mostrar siempre

Closes #1238

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: autopilot prompt improvements, token logging, and blueprint status decoupling

- Add role descriptions to autopilot prompt so LLM assigns proper roles when creating nodes
- Strengthen user message priority framing ("HIGHEST PRIORITY") with step-by-step guidance
- Add node creation guidelines (role assignment, dependency setup, decomposition patterns)
- Add LoggingRuntimeWrapper in agent-runtime.ts for prompt token/performance logging
- Add GET /api/agent-stats endpoint for aggregate call stats
- Auto-exit loop when all nodes done + no pending messages (prevents read_user_messages loop)
- Decouple blueprint status from autopilot — status is now user-managed only
  - Safeguards only set pauseReason, not status
  - pause/complete tools don't change blueprint status
  - Loop start doesn't set status to "running"
- Add BlueprintChat component and autopilot message system
- Add design docs for autopilot-centric refactor, chat interface, and dashboard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace base64 interpolation with stdin piping in all cloud exec_long functions (#2290)

Replace unsafe pattern where base64-encoded commands were interpolated
into remote command strings with secure stdin piping — command data now
travels as stdin rather than as part of the command string, eliminating
injection risk from shell metacharacter interpretation.

Affected functions across all 5 cloud drivers:
- _hetzner_exec_long
- _aws_exec_long
- _gcp_exec_long
- _digitalocean_exec_long
- _sprite_exec_long

Fixes #2286
Fixes #2287

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clean up Tambor drum sounds: remove noise, improve clarity and snappiness

Redesigned drum presets for cleaner, more defined electronic drum kit:
- Kick: Pure sine wave (noise 0.01) for round, clean bass
- Snare: Ultra-fast 0.01ms attack for crisp snap, 120ms decay for snappiness
- Closed HH: 0.02ms super-tight snap with clean shimmer
- Open HH: Cleaner sustain without harsh noise artifacts
- Clap: Faster attack for punchy transient
- Toms: Changed to pure sine with minimal noise (0.01) for focused tones

All drums now sound more electronic and defined with less muddiness.

commit.message:
Fix model listing: use Codex API instead of OpenAI /v1/models

The OpenAI /v1/models endpoint now requires api.model.read scope which
ChatGPT Plus OAuth tokens don't have. Switch to chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/models
which returns the actual available models (gpt-5.x series). Filter to
visible non-codex models for the settings UI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add table widget, per-column widths/alignment, line split, circle AA, and Spirix cleanup

- Table opcode {tb}: per-column widths (x tag, 0=hidden), per-column
  horizontal justify (j tag: l/c/r) and vertical alignment (v tag: t/m/b),
  auto-fit columns, per-row auto height from wrapped content, header/alt/border
  backgrounds, too-narrow sanity check
- Split line rendering into line_fast.rs and line_quality.rs
- Circle/ellipse anti-aliasing for both pipelines
- Remove all unnecessary ScalarF4E4::from(x as i32) casts — use direct
  Spirix primitive arithmetic (Scalar op usize/isize)
- Replace all Spirix * 2 / / 2 with << 1usize / >> 1usize (exponent bump
  instead of full multiply)
- Text vertical centering uses full text block height (descender to ascender
  for single line, layout height for wrapped)
- Builder: draw_table with per-column widths, alignment, optional styling
- Tag consistency: column width uses x (same concept as text wrap width),
  horizontal justify uses j, vertical alignment uses v

commit.message:
fix: measure actual KISO_DIR size instead of whole filesystem

shutil.disk_usage reports the entire mount point's usage, not the
target directory. On a host with 36 GB system usage and max_disk_gb=32,
every task was blocked despite kiso using <1 GB.

Replace with du -sb (fallback: os.walk) via _kiso_dir_bytes() shared
helper. sysenv.py now imports from worker/utils to avoid duplication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(webhooks): add webhook status subcommand with optional reachability check (#201)

Adds `raps webhook status` that lists all registered webhooks with their
event type, system, callback URL, APS status, and creation date in a
formatted health table. The `--check-reachability` flag makes a HEAD
request (5 s timeout) to each callback URL and reports reachable/unreachable.
A summary line shows active-webhook and reachable-URL counts.

commit.message:
feat(cli): operation history logging with raps history and raps replay (#199)

- Record every CLI invocation to ~/.cache/raps/history.json via record_history()
  using directories::ProjectDirs; skips history/replay subcommands themselves
- Appends {index, timestamp (RFC 3339), args} per entry; trims to last 100;
  writes atomically via .tmp rename
- New `raps history [--last N] [--json]` subcommand: prints a table of recent
  invocations with 1-based index, formatted timestamp, and full command string
- New `raps replay <index>` subcommand: looks up entry by 1-based index and
  re-executes it via std::process::Command, propagating the exit code

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(doctor): add token scope coverage and credential validity checks (#198)

- check_credential_validity: validates client_id/client_secret are non-empty
  and well-formed; warns when both env vars and an active profile are set
  simultaneously (potential config drift)
- check_token_scope_coverage: fetches a live 2-legged token and inspects
  the granted scopes against the six scopes required for common RAPS
  operations (bucket:read, bucket:create, data:read, data:write,
  data:create, viewables:read); emits a Warning per missing scope so
  users know which features will fail without erroring out unnecessarily

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(translate): auto-detect output format from input file extension (#196)

Inspects the URN's embedded filename extension and maps it to the
best output format (SVF2 for all supported types). Falls back to
interactive prompt when extension is unknown or URN can't be decoded.

Covers: .rvt .rfa .dwg .dxf .ipt .iam .ifc .stp .obj .stl .fbx .gltf and more.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Capitalize publication titles to title case on main page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stale service worker blocking push notifications on PWA

If existing SW fails to activate (stale precache), unregister it and
re-register fresh before subscribing to push. Improves error message
to guide users to clear site data as last resort.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(cli): fuzzy command suggestions for mistyped subcommands (#195)

When clap reports an unrecognized or invalid subcommand, extract the
mistyped word and compute Levenshtein distance against all known
top-level subcommands. If a match within distance ≤ 3 is found, print
"Did you mean: raps <suggestion>?" in yellow before the clap error.

No external crate added — Levenshtein is implemented inline.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: openHiTLS rewritten in Rust — a production-grade cryptographic and TLS library

commit.message:
fix: Block/unblock from chat, fix primary photo label on reorder, and fix duplicate peers in discovery

- Profile: reorderPhotos now updates isPrimary in the same transaction so
  the PRIMARY label always follows the first photo after drag-to-reorder

- Chat: enforce blocked status end-to-end — blocked peers cannot send or
  receive messages (BLE layer), chat input is replaced with a banner, and
  the ⋮ menu provides Block/Unblock with confirmation dialog

- Discovery: deduplicate visiblePeers by peerId as a safety net; retire
  stale BLE peer entries in FlutterBluePlusBleService when the same
  userId reappears under a new MAC (e.g. after restarting BLE advertising
  on primary-photo change), preventing the user from appearing twice

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(kernel): parse X-RateLimit headers and auto-throttle near quota (#194)

Add RateLimitState in raps-kernel/src/rate_limit.rs that parses
X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset from
every APS response. When remaining drops below 10% of the limit,
throttle_delay() returns a sleep duration (capped at 30 s) until the
window resets.

Wire it into send_with_retry in http.rs: after each successful response
the headers are parsed and, if near-quota, the coroutine sleeps before
the next outbound request. A debug log line is emitted for every
response that carries rate-limit headers so operators can observe quota
consumption in traces.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: two-level category navigation (group tabs + sub-category pills)

and sub-category pills below for cleaner navigation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(translate): add --watch flag for end-to-end translation workflow (#193)

Adds --watch, --poll-interval, and --watch-timeout flags to the
`raps translate start` command. When --watch is supplied the CLI
polls the manifest after submission until the job reaches a terminal
state (success / failed / timeout), displaying a spinner with live
status updates.

A new watch.rs module houses the reusable watch_translation helper
which calls DerivativeClient::get_status, honours a configurable
deadline (default 1800 s, 0 = no timeout), and propagates errors so
callers can act on failure.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(pipeline): add depends_on field with topological sort and cycle detection (#192)

- Add `depends_on: Vec<String>` field to Step struct for declarative ordering
- Add topological_sort() using Kahn's algorithm to resolve execution order
- Detect circular dependencies and report the cycle with a clear error message
- Validate all depends_on references point to known step names
- run_pipeline: resolve execution order before iterating steps
- run_pipeline: print resolved execution order in dry-run mode
- validate_pipeline: call topological_sort and report execution order on success,
  or add dep/cycle errors to the validation error list

commit.message:
Bot consensus + real Capital.com PnL

Autonomous mode: rotate all 4 bots every cycle (no API calls),
when 2+ bots agree on signal → ask IA to validate (1 API call).
This allows analysis every 30s without rate limits.

PnL: use Capital.com's real profit from order confirmation
instead of our calculated value. Fixes price discrepancy
between app (1.12€) and Capital.com (1.23€).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(oss): adaptive chunk sizing and ETA display for multipart uploads (#191)

- Add ThroughputTracker with 10-second rolling window for real-time
  bytes-per-second measurement during multipart uploads
- Display live throughput and ETA in the progress bar message:
  "Uploading <key> — X.X MB/s — ETA Xs"
- Add ThroughputCache that persists observed throughput to
  ~/.cache/raps/throughput_cache.json between runs
- Auto-select chunk size at upload start based on cached throughput:
  >50 MB/s → 25 MB, >10 MB/s → 10 MB, else 5 MB (default)
- Preserve DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE when resuming an interrupted upload

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Fixed agent-swap path to normalize array→string

- Added handling for "Hello?", "Who is this?" responses
- Agent now responds to target's questions before connecting
